etc. It is quite possible that there were many sages of that name in

(iv) The author states in LIV. 1252 that his authority for
'Rainfall'(XXIII) \s Baladevamatadi {views oSBaladeva and others).
However, the commentator does not quote him even once. On the
other hand, he quotes Garga thrice, Ka&yapa once, Devala once and
the Samdsa-samhitd twice (3 verses in all). It is, therefore, a subject
fit for investigation, both from the point of meteorology and author-
ship oIBaladeva.
(v) The author asserts that his authorities for temple architec-
ture are Garga, Manu and others (Maya, Vihakarman, etc.) ;3 but the
